changes.before: Eversong held around them in her usual way:
where scouts liked to wait when the south grew restless.
changes.after: and scouts liked to wait when raiders or smugglers stirred beyond the line.

changes.before: Knew too where the human lands started leaning against hers with all their usual untidiness.
changes.after: Human farms and roads crowded the border in their usual untidiness.

changes.before: She knew this stretch: where rain slicked the ground, where riders had to go single file, where scouts liked to wait when the south grew restless.
changes.after: Rain slicked the ground here, riders had to go single file, and scouts liked to wait when raiders or smugglers stirred beyond the line.

changes.before: The border never made her sentimental. It made her exact.
changes.after: At the border, she went exact.

changes.before: The border never made her sentimental. It made her exact.
changes.after: At the border, she went exact.

changes.before: The name did not need help landing.
changes.after: Sylvanas knew the name at once.

changes.before: If it was Jaina Proudmoore, that still did not make her honest. It only made her stranger than expected, and strange problems became personal if you left them alone.
changes.after: If it was Jaina Proudmoore, trust still had to be earned; the name only made her stranger than expected, and strange problems became personal if you left them alone.

changes.before: Wrong in every way that mattered.
changes.after: A field mage, then—travel-worn, split-knuckled, and miles past court polish.
